[2024-05-15 07:12] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I wanted to say what if it sniped >5 as a meme, but then decided it's not a great meme, but then it gave me an interesting idea

What if there was a mechanic that forced part of an attack to be non-granular

This non-granular attack could be called something like "Heavy" or "Siege" attack

Each instance of X Siege attack can only be defended using units whose health is >= X
-- EDIT: also it turns the chosen soak fragile, for example 3 Siege soaked on an infusion grid leaves you with a 1 HP fragile infusion grid (to solve the problem of multi-absorb making it useless, and force-killing >X hp soak making it OP AF)
-- EDIT: if no soak with >= X is available, the Siege attack breaches (as regular granular attack)

For example if you were being attacked for 10 + 2 instances of 3 Siege attack, then you could defend using 2 wall absorb + 8 engineers for the 10 part, and 2 walls for the 2*3 Siege part
[2024-05-15 07:13] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so if you are out of big soak, bye bye absorb
[2024-05-15 07:14] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): because for example if you have a matrix, then just one instance of 3 Siege can never bust it down
[2024-05-15 07:14] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but if there are two 3 Siege attackers, and you are completely out of big soak (that has 3 or more health), then you will be forced to put both 3 Sieges onto the matrix and it dies
[2024-05-15 07:15] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess there is the question of what happens to the overkill damage, I guess it can turn into granular attack, though it's a bit inelegant/complex
[2024-05-15 07:15] aa01blue: or just straight up waste it
[2024-05-15 07:16] aa01blue: which gives weird interactions where a 4 soak and a 5 soak do the same thing against 3 siege
[2024-05-15 07:17] aa01blue: then again it does the same thing against 3 chill
[2024-05-15 07:17] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but should the siege damage kill the soaking unit even if it has more health?
[2024-05-15 07:17] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I think it should be absorbed
[2024-05-15 07:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it's weird cos if it's absorbed then siege attackers or useless if there are blue health units with >X HP
[2024-05-15 07:18] aa01blue: Yeah but that causes weird interactions no
[2024-05-15 07:18] aa01blue: can you soak siege and damage separately
[2024-05-15 07:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): yea that's what I mean
[2024-05-15 07:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): if each instance of siege can be soaked as long as you have sufficient separate big HP units
[2024-05-15 07:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): then siege is kinda useless most of the time
[2024-05-15 07:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but the alternative is that each instance of siege kills the assigned soak
[2024-05-15 07:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): unless it is the last instance?
[2024-05-15 07:19] aa01blue: cuz like siege 3 is insane when wall biggest, but completely useless against igrids
[2024-05-15 07:19] aa01blue: or at least only as valuable as an igrid
[2024-05-15 07:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): yea if you allow multi absorb then it's useless
[2024-05-15 07:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and if you force kill each instance then it's busted OP
[2024-05-15 07:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): what if it turns the soak fragile?
[2024-05-15 07:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so it forces 0 absorb
[2024-05-15 07:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I think that's a decent solution
[2024-05-15 07:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): organic defense is immune to this siege tech which turns absorbers ~~brittle~~ fragile
[2024-05-15 07:22] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): cos organs are already fragile :LongNoseLiar:
[2024-05-15 07:22] aa01blue: I wouldn't say mahar is organic, I've always imagined it being forcefields
[2024-05-15 07:23] aa01blue: If you made each siege x its separate attack where you can assign blockers to block a multiple of x damage, it could work
[2024-05-15 07:24] aa01blue: assuming bigger siege better
[2024-05-15 07:24] aa01blue: as in you can only absorb siege 3 attacks with 1 unit, and siege 4 attacks with 1 unit, etc
[2024-05-15 07:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): yea I assume the implementation would require each siege to be its own attack
[2024-05-15 07:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): they would be resolved in turns
[2024-05-15 07:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): starting with the biggest siege I guess
[2024-05-15 07:25] aa01blue: then siege 2 + siege 3 might be worse than double siege 2 tho
[2024-05-15 07:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): well with the fragile version you can never absorb siege
[2024-05-15 07:25] aa01blue: against igrid for example
[2024-05-15 07:25] aa01blue: yeah but the fragile version is really sus
[2024-05-15 07:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I am going with the siege definition of: must choose a defending unit of >= X, and if >X then it takes X damage and turns fragile
[2024-05-15 07:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): if no option, then it breaches as standard granular attack
[2024-05-15 07:26] aa01blue: cuz of regen "absorber" interactions
[2024-05-15 07:27] aa01blue: would be funny if it must breach as nongranulsr attack
[2024-05-15 07:28] aa01blue: i mean it's already a wall killer
[2024-05-15 07:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): you have strictly less (or at most equal) options on what you can assign the siege3 attack onto as the defender
[2024-05-15 07:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so how can it ever be better for the defender
[2024-05-15 07:30] aa01blue: if we assume you can only absorb using 1 defender against all instances of siege x
[2024-05-15 07:30] aa01blue: 2 igrids can absorb both siege 2 and 3
[2024-05-15 07:30] aa01blue: but you have to kill off an igrid to absorb 2 siege 2
[2024-05-15 07:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): well in the fragile version there is no longer the overkill being wasted though
[2024-05-15 07:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): there is never overkill in the fragilemaker version

[2024-05-15 07:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): unless you breach and there is nothing to kill

[2024-05-15 07:37] aa01blue: shame that it gets invalidated by the goated plexo cell

[2024-05-15 07:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it would force a partial breach in some sets
[2024-05-15 07:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and not for a massive amount
[2024-05-15 07:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): which is interesting
[2024-05-15 07:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): perhaps you can make it so that if there is a breaching siege, it first goes through the biggest soak available
[2024-05-15 07:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or does that break sdr
[2024-05-15 07:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I don't think it does
[2024-05-15 07:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): since the attacker can choose to kill that thing on breach anyway
[2024-05-15 07:39] aa01blue: who chooses which of the biggest soaks available
[2024-05-15 07:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): since in siege breaching there will still be leftover defense sometimes, which the attacker should be able to target under this new mechanic
[2024-05-15 07:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): otherwise you break sdr
[2024-05-15 07:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so I think it's fine to force target the biggest soak on breaching siege to soften the siege breach
[2024-05-15 07:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and I think it makes sense thematically
[2024-05-15 07:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): since the defender would sack their biggest soak to soften the blow
[2024-05-15 07:40] aa01blue: ig turning into regular attack if not defended fixes sdr?
[2024-05-15 07:42] aa01blue: I thought the idea was that the siege would just pummel through defenders smaller than the attack
[2024-05-15 07:42] aa01blue: otherwise what's stopping the defender from throwing engis at it
[2024-05-15 07:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): yea I'm settling on this definition:

"Siege" is non-granular attack (cannot be defended "granularly") that turns defense fragile and can only be soaked using sufficiently high health units, otherwise it forcefully breaches (after being softened by the biggest available soak)

Siege X: 

- Each instance of Siege will be resolved in turns, before the main "normal" attack, and starting with the largest instances of Siege attack first

- For each instance of Siege X, the defender must assign soak with health >= X -- and if it is >X, it turns fragile and takes X damage

- If there is no soak with health >= X, then the attacker must choose one of the biggest health soak available, and then gets to breach with the leftover damage as normal granular attack
(in this case, this "forced Siege breach" happens before the attacker passes their turn, just as regular defense overrun breach would)
--> For example: Siege3 -- nothing with >= 3 health --> attacker has to choose between a forcefield and a rhino (both of which have 2 health and are the highest health soak currently on the defender's side), and then gets to breach with the leftover 1 attack
[2024-05-15 07:45] aa01blue: it's pretty interesting
[2024-05-15 07:46] aa01blue: I think it's a bit complicated to fit in with the other prismata mechanics but it sounds like it could lead to interactions

[2024-05-15 07:59] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ok I think there's a flaw in this definition
[2024-05-15 07:59] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): a breach could happen AFTER the attacker passes their turn
[2024-05-15 08:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so I think I must alter the definition such that ALL siege happens at the start of the attacker's turn
[2024-05-15 08:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): just like regular breaches
[2024-05-15 08:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): oh but this breaks the mechanic
[2024-05-15 08:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): cos then you can just kill the cent for free
[2024-05-15 08:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): then not sure off the cuff how to solve this issue

[2024-05-15 08:01] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): to the point where I think the mechanic is not viable
[2024-05-15 08:02] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and you can't make it so that the attacker must choose from the smallest units >= X either
[2024-05-15 08:02] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): because it breaks SDR
[2024-05-15 08:03] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or at least, it makes it OP again

[2024-05-15 08:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but yea, in its current form it kinda sux cos you could get scenarios where you pass your turn as the attacker with say two Siege3s, the defender can soak one of them but the other breaches

so it goes:
- attacker ends turn
- defender soaks one siege
- attacker breaches with the other siege
- defender continues the remaining standard defense phase and then takes their turn
- ...

[2024-05-17 17:46] surfinite: Here's something I was thinking about during a long car journey:
What would be the go to strategy if every unit in the game could be bought?  I imagine it would be very Rock-Paper-Scissors-y, where reacting to your opponent becomes even more important.
If one or a few key units would always be played towards, then how many units do you need need to ban before you could end up with a wildly different strategy winning every game? (And what units get banned?)

[2024-06-29 20:07] flower6149 (Flower): plexo cells and bloodphages are stronger that i thought they would be
[2024-06-29 20:08] iminabearsuit: Yeah, Plexo is really strong. Feral Warden also is just crazy value, if you can defend on them.
[2024-06-29 20:09] iminabearsuit: Feral Warden is the same attack and cost as a tarsier + gauss charge. So, if you can additionally get 2 absorb onto it, its just one of the most efficient things you can be doing.
[2024-06-29 20:10] iminabearsuit: And this game, you ended up just a turn after me in getting all the good units there.
[2024-06-29 20:11] flower6149 (Flower): its fun how that works
[2024-06-29 20:11] flower6149 (Flower): why did you get a 3rd bloodphage?
[2024-06-29 20:13] iminabearsuit: Well, blood phage is kind of close to drone tarsier. 6gold vs 7gold. But blood phage "loses" 1 gold next turn. But in that spot I needed an extra engineer to be able to have a good defense
[2024-06-29 20:13] iminabearsuit: So I only had 6 gold left
[2024-06-29 20:16] flower6149 (Flower): I will start thinking of things in “drone-tarsier” terms
[2024-06-29 20:16] iminabearsuit: Yeah, it simplifies comparrisions
[2024-06-29 20:16] flower6149 (Flower): thx for the games @iminaBearSuit and @zezetel
[2024-06-29 20:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea it's really strong, but just as a note, it isn't technically 2 absorb

It's 2 soak
[2024-06-29 20:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Like if I absorb 2 on a wall, next turn I need to defend 2 less compared to if I soaked 2 on a feral
[2024-06-29 20:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): (Because the wall is still there participating on defense, whereas the feral would be swinging on 1 health in the backline)
[2024-06-29 20:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Which is why in small red feral vs feral there sometimes comes a point where it's better to full soak feral and absorb 1 on rhino
[2024-06-29 20:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): For that +1 defense on the following turn
[2024-06-29 20:22] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): (Though idk, usually the side who has to do that first is losing? Not sure -- but either way, it's more defense)

[2024-06-29 20:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea no worries! Not sure either
Just wanted to clarify that it's not the same as "absorbing" 2 (such as on a Wall) -- in case that's a potential point of confusion for newer players with the terminology

I generally say something like "defend on" to indicate which unit I assigned the final bit of incoming attack to

And "absorb" for when that unit has non-fragile :Health: HP (or regen like Mahar / Xae) -- so "free" defense tempo was gained

And "soak" for soaked units (that fully died as part of defense), as well as for lost fragile health on the defended upon unit (if it has non-regenerating fragile :Fragile: HP, like in the Feral example)
